text.title: continuous-delivery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for continuous-delivery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Large batch sizes and manual testing causing bottlenecks prevent frequent deployments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The release coordinator becomes a bottleneck. Manual testing is unreliable and slow. Multiple changes interact unpredictably, making rollback hard.

failure-mode.description: Testing takes too long, blocking the pipeline from flowing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serial test execution wastes time. E2E tests are often brittle and slow. Flaky tests are retried, adding latency.

failure-mode.description: Database migrations break backward compatibility, causing downtime during deployment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-backward-compatible migrations (adding NOT NULL columns, renaming tables without aliases) conflict with the previous version still running during rolling deploys.

failure-mode.description: Agents invent GitHub Actions or Argo Rollouts fields that don't exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate plausible-sounding YAML schemas. Invalid YAML passes local testing but fails in CI.

failure-mode.description: Agents couple database migrations and app deployment in one job.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the app crashes after migration but before rollback is called, the migration is not rolled back. Data is now in a future schema the old app cannot read.

failure-mode.description: Agents emit a deploy job and forget the rollback step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deployment fails silently or partially. Manual intervention required at 3 a.m. Always require an `if: failure()` rollback step or equivalent.

failure-mode.description: Smoke tests only check the /health endpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 200 OK on /health doesn't prove checkout works. The app could have crashed the critical path and still be technically "healthy."

failure-mode.description: Agents add feature flags without an expiration date or owner.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flags accumulate. After a year, 200 stale flags litter the codebase. Developers don't know what's live.

failure-mode.description: Multiple pull requests merge to main simultaneously, causing parallel deploys.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two deploys race. The second one overwrites the first without knowing it happened. Use concurrency locks per environment.
